## 3. DESIGN DECISIONS & PATTERNS

### 3.1 Architecture Decision Records (architecture-decisions.md)

**ADR-001: PixiJS v8 for WebGL** (NOT in final!)
- **Rejected**: DOM/SVG rendering inadequate for 500k+ data points
- **Choice**: PixiJS v8 (most performant 2D WebGL engine)
- **Note**: Later evolved to React Three Fiber in final implementation

**ADR-002: pixi-viewport for Navigation**
- **Pattern**: Google Maps-style pan/zoom with inertia
- **Physics**: Native smooth zoom, pinch-to-zoom, momentum
- **Alternative**: Custom physics (too expensive)

**ADR-003: Virtual Scrolling (Math-based)**
- **Rejected**: Ghost Div Pattern (DOM scrolling)
- **Rejected**: react-window, react-virtualized
- **Choice**: Physics-based virtual scroller for seamless fractal zoom

**ADR-004: Structure of Arrays (SoA) with TypedArrays**
- **Pattern**: Separate Float64Array, Uint16Array per property
- **Benefit**: Zero GC during interaction, cache locality
- **Tradeoff**: Verbose code, harder debugging
- **Code example**: `startMs: Float64Array`, `aircraftIndex: Uint16Array`

**ADR-005: @belt/tools State Machine (NOT React Hooks)**
- **Rejected**: useState/useRef/useEffect (stale closures)
- **Rejected**: Zustand (mutable state issues)
- **Choice**: @belt/tools with `useSyncExternalStore`
- **Benefit**: Synchronous state access, works in animation loops

**ADR-006: Vite SPA** (NOT Next.js)
- **Rejected**: Astro, Next.js (no SSR needed)
- **Choice**: Vite 6 for minimal, fast dev server

### 3.2 Code Patterns (code-patterns.md - 60k lines of reference)

**Working Pattern: External Store + useSyncExternalStore**

```typescript
// viewport.ts
class ViewportStore {
  private state: ViewportState;
  private listeners: Set<Listener>;
  
  getState = (): ViewportState => this.state;
  subscribe = (listener: Listener) => { /* ... */ };
  
  pan = (dx: number, dy: number) => { /* update state */ };
  zoomIn = () => { /* update state */ };
}

export const viewportStore = new ViewportStore();

export function useViewport() {
  const state = useSyncExternalStore(viewportStore.subscribe, viewportStore.getState);
  return { state, actions: { pan, zoomIn, ... } };
}
```

**Benefit**: State always synchronous, no stale closures, works in `useFrame` loops

**Working Pattern: Structure of Arrays (SoA)**

```typescript
// mockData.ts
export interface TaskSoA {
  count: number;
  ids: string[];
  trainIds: Uint16Array;      // Per-task train index
  startDays: Uint32Array;     // Per-task start day
  durations: Uint32Array;     // Per-task duration
  types: Uint8Array;          // Per-task type (0=preventive, 1=corrective...)
}

export function tasksToSoA(tasks: Task[]): TaskSoA {
  const soa: TaskSoA = {
    count: tasks.length,
    ids: tasks.map(t => t.id),
    trainIds: new Uint16Array(tasks.map(t => t.trainId)),
    startDays: new Uint32Array(tasks.map(t => t.startDay)),
    durations: new Uint32Array(tasks.map(t => t.duration)),
    types: new Uint8Array(tasks.map(t => TASK_TYPE_INDEX[t.type])),
  };
  return soa;
}
```

**Benefit**: Contiguous memory, fast iteration, minimal GC

**Working Pattern: InstancedMesh for Rendering**

```typescript
// MesoLayer.tsx - Task blocks using InstancedMesh
function TaskBlocks({ tasks, conflictIndices }: TaskBlocksProps) {
  const meshRef = useRef<THREE.InstancedMesh>(null);
  
  const { matrices, colors } = useMemo(() => {
    const matrices = new Float32Array(tasks.count * 16);
    const colors = new Float32Array(tasks.count * 3);
    
    for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {
      // Position: x = startDay * DAY_WIDTH + width/2
      const x = tasks.startDays[i]! * DAY_WIDTH + ...
      // Color: conflict? red : task type color
      const color = conflictIndices?.has(i) ? CONFLICT_COLOR : typeColors[...];
      // Store in arrays
      dummy.matrix.toArray(matrices, i * 16);
      colors[i * 3] = color[0]; // R
      colors[i * 3 + 1] = color[1]; // G
      colors[i * 3 + 2] = color[2]; // B
    }
    return { matrices, colors };
  }, [tasks, conflictIndices]);
  
  // Apply to mesh once mounted
  useEffect(() => {
    const mesh = meshRef.current;
    for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {
      const matrix = new THREE.Matrix4();
      matrix.fromArray(matrices, i * 16);
      mesh.setMatrixAt(i, matrix);
    }
    mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true;
    mesh.instanceColor.array = colors;
    mesh.instanceColor.needsUpdate = true;
  }, [matrices, colors]);
  
  return <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[geometry, material, tasks.count]} />;
}
```

**Benefit**: 60k tasks rendered in single draw call; GPU-accelerated batching

**Working Pattern: LOD Transitions with Smoothstep**

```typescript
// viewport.ts
export function getLODOpacity(zoom: number) {
  const smoothstep = (edge0: number, edge1: number, x: number) => {
    const t = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (x - edge0) / (edge1 - edge0)));
    return t * t * (3 - 2 * t); // Hermite interpolation
  };
  
  let macro = 0, meso = 0, micro = 0;
  
  if (zoom < 0.425) {
    macro = 1;
  } else if (zoom < 0.575) {
    // Crossfade zone (15% hysteresis)
    macro = 1 - smoothstep(0.425, 0.575, zoom);
    meso = smoothstep(0.425, 0.575, zoom);
  } else if (zoom < 1.7) {
    meso = 1;
  } else if (zoom < 2.3) {
    // Crossfade zone
    meso = 1 - smoothstep(1.7, 2.3, zoom);
    micro = smoothstep(1.7, 2.3, zoom);
  } else {
    micro = 1;
  }
  
  return { macro, meso, micro };
}
```

**Benefit**: Smooth crossfades between LOD layers; Hermite easing for visual polish

**Anti-Pattern: Avoid**
- ❌ useState in animation loops (stale closures)
- ❌ useEffect for pan/zoom (update lag)
- ❌ Array of Objects (cache miss locality)
- ❌ Zustand for high-frequency updates (batching issues)
- ❌ DOM rendering for 10k+ items (reflow/repaint)

## 4. USEFUL CODE PATTERNS & IMPLEMENTATIONS

### 4.1 Viewport/Camera Management

**Pattern**: World <-> Screen coordinate conversion
```typescript
export function worldToScreen(
  worldX: number, worldY: number, viewport: ViewportState
): { x: number; y: number } {
  const x = (worldX - viewport.x) * viewport.zoom + viewport.width / 2;
  const y = (worldY - viewport.y) * viewport.zoom + viewport.height / 2;
  return { x, y };
}
```

**Usage**: Positioning UI overlays on canvas content

### 4.2 Conflict Detection Algorithm

```typescript
// conflictDetection.ts
export function detectConflicts(tasks: TaskSoA): Conflict[] {
  const conflicts: Conflict[] = [];
  
  for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {
    for (let j = i + 1; j < tasks.count; j++) {
      // Same train, overlapping time?
      if (tasks.trainIds[i] === tasks.trainIds[j]) {
        const taskAEnd = tasks.startDays[i] + tasks.durations[i];
        const taskBEnd = tasks.startDays[j] + tasks.durations[j];
        
        if (!(taskAEnd <= tasks.startDays[j] || taskBEnd <= tasks.startDays[i])) {
          conflicts.push({ taskA: i, taskB: j });
        }
      }
    }
  }
  return conflicts;
}
```

```typescript
// mockData.ts
function seededRandom(seed: number) {

    seed = (seed * 1103515245 + 12345) & 0x7fffffff;
    return seed / 0x7fffffff;
  };
}

export function generateMockTasks(

): Task[] {
  const random = seededRandom(42); // Same seed = same data
  // ... generate 128 * 12 * 4 ≈ 6k tasks
}
```

**Benefit**: Reproducible test data across sessions

## 7. DESIGN SYSTEM CHOICES & UI PATTERNS

**PatternFly 6 Component Usage:**

**Spacing System (4px base):**
- 8px between toolbar items
- 12px padding in cards
- 130px sidebar width (collapsed), 280px (expanded)

**Typography Hierarchy:**

**Interactive Patterns:**
- Zoom buttons (linear control)
- Mode toggle (blue/violet background swap)
- LOD indicator (color-coded: orange/blue/green)
- Theme toggle (sun/moon icon)

## 8. REUSABLE PATTERNS FOR MAIN PROJECT

### Immediately Reusable:

1. **Viewport Store Pattern** (`stores/viewport.ts`)
   - Camera position, zoom, bounds
   - useSyncExternalStore pattern
   - World ↔ screen coordinate conversion

2. **Structure of Arrays (SoA)** (`utils/mockData.ts`)
   - TypedArray-based data structure
   - Memory-efficient for 60k+ items
   - Fast iteration for rendering

3. **InstancedMesh Pattern** (`render/MesoLayer.tsx`)
   - Batch rendering 60k task blocks
   - Per-instance color via InstancedBufferAttribute
   - Per-instance transforms via matrix array

4. **LOD System** (`viewport.ts`)
   - Smooth crossfade transitions (Hermite smoothstep)
   - Hysteresis zones to prevent flicker
   - Three LOD levels: MACRO/MESO/MICRO

5. **Conflict Detection** (`utils/conflictDetection.ts`)
   - O(n²) scan for time-overlapping tasks on same train
   - Efficient for 60k tasks in mock scenario

6. **Design System** (`design-system.md`)
   - CSS custom properties for theming
   - Color palette with semantic tokens
   - Spacing and typography scales

7. **Harness System** (`progress.txt`, `features.json`)
   - Session continuity tracking
   - Feature passing/failing status
   - Multi-phase sprint organization

### Strategic Insights:

1. **Never trust DOM rendering** for 10k+ items (use WebGL/canvas)
2. **External stores** (not hooks) for animation loop synchronization
3. **Separate data structure** from rendering (SoA separate from Three.js)
4. **Competitor analysis** reveals genuine user needs (Marey charts, resource histograms)
5. **Domain unknowns** (statutory tasks, bay limits, disruption mode) require proactive research
6. **3-layer sandwich** (engine/canvas/HUD) keeps concerns cleanly separated
7. **LOD with hysteresis** prevents jarring transitions during continuous zoom

## Phase 4: Design System Enforcement

### 4.1 Create Design System Hook
**File:** `.claude/hooks/enforce-design-system.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Enforce BlueprintJS design system
# Block: custom colors, non-BP components, arbitrary spacing

BLOCKED_PATTERNS=(
  "style={{.*color:"
  "style={{.*background:"
  "import.*from.*@patternfly"
  "import.*from.*@mui"
  "import.*from.*antd"
)

for pattern in "${BLOCKED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
  if grep -rE "$pattern" apps/canvas/src/ 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "BLOCKED: Design system violation - use BlueprintJS tokens"
    exit 1
  fi
done
```

```markdown
## Design System (BlueprintJS 6)

### Color Usage
- Use Blueprint Intent colors: PRIMARY, SUCCESS, WARNING, DANGER
- Dark theme tokens: `bp6-dark` class
- Task types: PREVENT (green), CORRECT (orange), OVERHAUL (red)

### Component Mapping
| Need | BlueprintJS Component |
|------|----------------------|
| Navbar | `<Navbar>` + `<NavbarGroup>` |
| Sidebar | `<Card>` with elevation |
| Buttons | `<Button>` / `<ButtonGroup>` |
| Tags | `<Tag>` with intent |
| Panels | `<Card>` / `<Drawer>` |
| Inputs | `<InputGroup>` |
| Selects | `<Select>` / `<HTMLSelect>` |

### Spacing Scale (4px base)
- xs: 4px, sm: 8px, md: 12px, lg: 16px, xl: 24px

### Typography
- Use Blueprint's built-in typography classes
- Monospace for train IDs: `bp6-monospace-text`
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Enforce BlueprintJS design system

BLOCKED_PATTERNS=(
  "style={{.*color:"
  "style={{.*background:"
  "import.*from.*@patternfly"
  "import.*from.*@mui"
  "import.*from.*antd"
)

for pattern in "${BLOCKED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do

    exit 1
  fi
done
```

```markdown
## Design System (BlueprintJS 6)

### Color Usage
- Use Blueprint Intent colors: PRIMARY, SUCCESS, WARNING, DANGER
```

     2→ * InfiniteCanvas - Professional-grade pan/zoom canvas
     3→ *
     4→ * Features:
     5→ * - Pan with mouse drag (bounded to world limits)

     9→ *

    11→ * - X: 0 = first day, positive = future (right)
    12→ * - Y: 0 = first train (TRAIN-001), negative = more trains (down)
    13→ *
    14→ * References:
    15→ * - D3.js zoom: https://d3js.org/d3-zoom
    16→ * - Three.js OrthographicCamera: https://threejs.org/docs/api/en/cameras/OrthographicCamera.html

    "content": "     1→# UX Competitor Analysis - Railway Maintenance Scheduling Software
     2→
     3→> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-08
     4→> **Purpose**: Steal best ideas from competitors - "Good artists copy, great artists steal"
     5→> **Sources**: Web research via Anthropic Claude
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. IBM Maximo Scheduler
    10→
    11→### Overview
    12→IBM's enterprise asset management system, widely used in rail/metro operations including Dubai Metro.
    13→
    14→### Key UI Features
    15→
    16→#### Gantt Chart Capabilities
    17→- **Drag-and-drop scheduling**: Modify and mass-update work orders directly in Gantt view
    18→- **Task dependencies**: Visual critical path with float calculation
    19→- **Resource histogram**: Below Gantt showing resource loading vs availability
    20→- **Multiple views**: Day, week, month, quarter, year timeline views
    21→- **PM forecasting**: Gantt view for preventive maintenance with easy drag-drop adjustments
    22→
    23→#### Planning Views
    24→- **Graphical Scheduling**: Long to short term planning with Gantt + histogram
    25→- **Work View & Assignment View**: Both Gantt-based for supervisors
    26→- **Dispatch View**: Day calendar + map showing labor/crew assignments with travel time
    27→
    28→#### Railway-Specific Features
    29→- **Consist management**: For railway assets
    30→- **Dynamic segmentation**: For Railways, Roads, Pipelines
    31→- **Repair facility assignment**: Short-term planning for garage/railway shed assignment
    32→
    33→### Prometheus Group Enhancement (for Maximo)
    34→- **Four-quadrant Gantt layout**: Flexible view
    35→- **Real-time capacity effects**: See resource impact instantly
    36→- **Direct Edit or Mass Change**: For orders and operations
    37→
    38→### Weaknesses to Exploit
    39→- Fragmented into role-based apps (our opportunity: unified interface)
    40→- Struggles with 10k+ records (our target: 60fps with 60k tasks)
    41→- Discrete day/week/month tabs (our advantage: continuous semantic zoom)
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## 2. Railnova (Brussels, Belgium)
    46→
    47→### Overview
    48→Fleet management + telematics for rail industry. Clients include Eurotunnel, Alstom, Lineas, SNCF, DB Cargo.
    49→
    50→### Products
    51→
    52→#### Railfleet (ECM Fleet Management)
    53→- **Operational Dashboard**: All fleet info in one place
    54→  - Asset status
    55→  - Restrictions
    56→  - Rolling stock location
    57→  - Key telematic values
    58→  - Events and availability notifications
    59→
    60→- **Planning Dashboard**: Complete overview of maintenance events
    61→  - Event states: unplanned, planned, overdue, closed
    62→  - Per-asset filtering
    63→  - Bundle corrective + preventive into interventions
    64→
    65→- **Maintenance Planning Dashboard**:
    66→  - Filter events by type
    67→  - Schedule new maintenance interventions
    68→  - Bundle based on open events
    69→
    70→- **Digital Train Logbook**:
    71→  - Real-time tablet view for drivers
    72→  - Train availability and condition
    73→  - Operational status and restrictions
    74→  - Recent events list
    75→
    76→#### Railgenius (Real-time Monitoring)
    77→- **Live Monitoring Module**:
    78→  - Real-time telematics from all assets
    79→  - Color-coded values for anomaly detection
    80→  - Predictive alerts via detection rules
    81→
    82→- **Telematics Data Dashboard**:
    83→  - Historical and real-time data
    84→  - Usage pattern analysis
    85→  - Fault code occurrence reporting
    86→
    87→### Integration Capabilities
    88→- Web services API
    89→- IBM Maximo, MySQL Server, .Net, Oracle, SAP integration
    90→
    91→### UI Patterns to Steal
    92→- Color-coded anomaly indicators (spot abnormal values instantly)
    93→- Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)
    94→- Digital logbook concept for train-level status
    95→- Bundling corrective + preventive events
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## 3. SignatureRail (UK, Modaxo Group)
   100→
   101→### Overview
   102→Train timetable and resource planning since 1994. Global presence with TrainPlan flagship product.
   103→
   104→### TrainPlan Features
   105→- Timetable generation considering all resources and constraints
   106→- Optimize capacity
   107→- Reduce vehicle and operator costs
   108→- Improve services
   109→
   110→### User Testimonial (Metro-North Railroad)
   111→> "TrainPlan allows us to manage our data in an efficient manner allowing for safe and high performing service changes to be implemented as quickly as possible."
   112→
   113→### Marey Chart Visualization (Industry Gold Standard)
   114→
   115→#### What is a Marey Chart?
   116→- Invented by Étienne-Jules Marey (1878) based on Mr. Ibry's method
   117→- **Axes**: Distance (Y) vs Time (X)
   118→- **Trains**: Diagonal lines - slope = speed
   119→- **Direction**: Top-left to bottom-right (one direction), bottom-left to top-right (reverse)
   120→- **Stops**: Horizontal sections indicate stop duration
   121→- **Crossings**: Lines crossing = trains passing each other
   122→
   123→#### Why Marey Charts are Superior
   124→- "Far superior representation for finding late trains and understanding delay propagation"
   125→- Both time and space dimensions in compact form
   126→- Used as cover of Tufte's "The Visual Display of Quantitative Information"
   127→- Speed immediately visible from line slope
   128→
   129→#### Modern Implementations
   130→- [D3.js Observable Example](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)
   131→- [JointJS Demo](https://www.jointjs.com/demos/marey-chart)
   132→- [Tableau Public](https://public.tableau.com/en-us/s/blog/2015/08/marey-inspired-path-train-schedule)
   133→
   134→### UI Patterns to Steal
   135→- Marey chart for train scheduling visualization
   136→- What-if scenario management
   137→- Constraint-aware scheduling
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→## 4. Other Notable Competitors
   142→
   143→### PTV Visum
   144→- **Scenario management**: Create and manage infrastructure modifications
   145→- **Fleet planning**: Depot capacities, turning times, empty runs
   146→- **Batch evaluation**: Compare scenarios programmatically
   147→
   148→### TBEST (Free Desktop Software)
   149→- **GIS-based**: Integrates socio-economic, land use, transit network
   150→- **Scenario planning**: True network-based future year simulation
   151→
   152→### AnyLogic
   153→- **Simulation-based optimization**: Two-layer approach
   154→- **What-if scenarios**: Assess economic effects of changes
   155→- **Replacement service planning**: For scheduled shutdowns
   156→
   157→### Tractian CMMS
   158→- **AI-driven workload balancing**: Eliminates scheduling conflicts
   159→- **Real-time technician availability**: Auto task prioritization
   160→- **Visual Scheduling**: Plan by technician, area, or shift
   161→- **Overload detection**: See idle time instantly
   162→
   163→### FaultFixers
   164→- **Drag-and-drop calendar**: Intuitive ticket assignment
   165→- **Gantt Chart view**: See everyone's schedule at once
   166→
   167→---
   168→
   169→## 5. Transit Dashboard Best Practices
   170→
   171→### Real-World Examples
   172→
   173→#### WMATA (Washington Metro)
   174→- **MetroPulse**: Real-time headway and schedule adherence
   175→- **Trains in service**: Live count
   176→- **Service Excellence Dashboard**: Rail, Bus, MetroAccess data
   177→
   178→#### CTE KPI Platform
   179→- **Single pane of glass**: Track progress, spot trends
   180→- **Automated API feeds**: Vehicle telematics integration
   181→- **Custom data inputs**: Flexible reporting
   182→
   183→### Common Transit KPIs
   184→- Miles driven
   185→- Drop sequence
   186→- Number of stops
   187→- Arrival/departure times
   188→- DOT reportable incidents
   189→- Speeding incidents
   190→- Cost-per-mile
   191→- Revenue-per-mile
   192→- Miles per gallon
   193→- Vehicle idle time
   194→- On-time performance
   195→- Missed trips
   196→- Prediction accuracy
   197→
   198→---
   199→
   200→## 6. Gestalt Principles for Dashboard Design
   201→
   202→### Core Principles
   203→
   204→1. **Proximity**
   205→   - Group related filters together
   206→   - Place filter near its affected view
   207→   - Example: Fleet status filters grouped with fleet list
   208→
   209→2. **Similarity**
   210→   - Same colors for same data types
   211→   - Consistent shapes for similar metrics
   212→   - Example: All "fit" trains in green, all "unfit" in red
   213→
   214→3. **Enclosure**
   215→   - Cards/boxes for related metrics
   216→   - Clear distinction between data sets
   217→   - Example: KPI cards with distinct borders
   218→
   219→4. **Connectedness**
   220→   - Lines linking related elements
   221→   - Network and line graph patterns
   222→   - Example: Dependency lines between tasks
   223→
   224→5. **Closure**
   225→   - Minimal axis lines (just X and Y enough)
   226→   - User perceives complete structure
   227→   - Example: Bar charts without full enclosure
   228→
   229→6. **Continuity**
   230→   - Sort bars large to small
   231→   - Time charts past to future
   232→   - Perceived as continuous whole
   233→
   234→### Application to MMS
   235→- Group maintenance metrics together (proximity)
   236→- Color-code by fleet type and task status (similarity)
   237→- Use cards for KPIs with clear borders (enclosure)
   238→- Show task dependencies with connecting lines (connectedness)
   239→- Minimal chart decoration (closure)
   240→- Chronological timeline (continuity)
   241→
   242→---
   243→
   244→## 7. Calendar Heatmap Patterns
   245→
   246→### When to Use
   247→- Daily values are important
   248→- Want to view whole year at once
   249→- Spot patterns at month/week/day level
   250→- Compare weekdays vs weekends
   251→
   252→### When NOT to Use
   253→- Need trend/seasonality analysis (use line charts)
   254→- Monthly/yearly aggregate trends
   255→- Simple KPI tracking
   256→
   257→### Best Practices
   258→- Choose readable colors even with dark shades
   259→- Use continuous date calendar table
   260→- Common layouts: 4x3, 3x4, 6x2 grids
   261→- Color intensity = value magnitude
   262→
   263→### MMS Application
   264→- **MACRO view**: Calendar heatmap showing maintenance density
   265→- **Color scale**: Viridis (low=yellow → high=purple)
   266→- **Drill-down**: Click month/week to zoom MESO level
   267→
   268→---
   269→
   270→## 8. MMS Differentiators (vs Competitors)
   271→
   272→| Feature | Competitors | MMS |
   273→|---------|-------------|-----|
   274→| Zoom | Discrete tabs (day/week/month) | Continuous semantic zoom |
   275→| Performance | Maximo struggles at 10k | 60fps with 60k tasks |
   276→| Interface | Fragmented role-based apps | Unified single canvas |
   277→| Conflict resolution | Manual identification | Visual drag-drop with instant feedback |
   278→| Scenario mode | Separate planning modules | Inline sandbox with violet visual |
   279→| Chart type | Gantt only | Gantt + Marey + Heatmap (LOD) |
   280→
   281→---
   282→
   283→## 9. Features to Steal (Priority Order)
   284→
   285→### HIGH PRIORITY
   286→1. Drag-and-drop Gantt scheduling (IBM Maximo, Tractian)
   287→2. Color-coded anomaly indicators (Railnova)
   288→3. Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)
   289→4. Resource histogram below schedule (Maximo)
   290→5. Marey chart for train visualization (SignatureRail)
   291→
   292→### MEDIUM PRIORITY
   293→6. Digital train logbook per asset (Railnova)
   294→7. Four-quadrant flexible layout (Prometheus)
   295→8. Scenario comparison with batch evaluation (PTV Visum)
   296→9. What-if scenario with economic impact (AnyLogic)
   297→10. Calendar heatmap for maintenance density
   298→
   299→### FUTURE CONSIDERATION
   300→11. AI-driven workload balancing (Tractian)
   301→12. GIS integration (TBEST)
   302→13. Predictive maintenance alerts (Railgenius)
   303→14. Mobile dispatch interface (Railnova)
   304→
   305→---
   306→

   308→

   310→- [Railnova Official Site](https://www.railnova.eu/)
   311→- [Railfleet ECM Platform](https://www.railnova.eu/products/railfleet/)
   312→- [SignatureRail Planning Solutions](https://signaturerail.com/planning-solutions/)
   313→- [Marey's Trains - D3 Observable](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)
   314→- [Gestalt Principles in Dashboard Design](https://playfairdata.com/applying-gestalt-principles-to-dashboard-design/)
   315→- [WMATA Service Excellence Dashboard](https://www.wmata.com/initiatives/open-data-hub/Metro-Service-Excellence-Dashboard.cfm)
   316→- [Tractian CMMS](https://tractian.com/en/cmms/planning-and-scheduling)

    "content": "     1→# MMS V1.0 GENESIS
     2→
     3→> Metro Mileage Management System - High-performance visualization for railway fleet maintenance planning.
     4→
     5→## Quick Start
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→# Install dependencies
     9→bun install
    10→
    11→# Run development server
    12→cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
    13→
    14→# Open http://localhost:5173
    15→```
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## HARNESS: Read This First
    20→
    21→This project uses the **Harness** methodology for AI-assisted development.
    22→
    23→**What is a Harness?** A framework that enables AI agents to work across multiple sessions by persisting state to files.
    24→
    25→**Reference:** [Anthropic - Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
    26→
    27→### State Files
    28→
    29→| File | Purpose |
    30→|------|---------|
    31→| `progress.txt` | Session history - **READ FIRST** |
    32→| `features.json` | Feature status (passes: true/false) |
    33→| `docs/SPECIFICATION.md` | **AUTHORITATIVE** technical specification |
    34→
    35→### Work Protocol
    36→
    37→```
    38→1. READ progress.txt
    39→2. READ features.json (find next feature with passes: false)
    40→3. IMPLEMENT one feature
    41→4. TEST visually (Playwright MCP)
    42→5. COMMIT after passing
    43→6. UPDATE progress.txt
    44→```
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Tech Stack (V1.0)
    49→
    50→| Technology | Version | Purpose |
    51→|------------|---------|---------|
    52→| React | 18.3.1 | UI framework |
    53→| Vite | 5.x | Build tool |
    54→| React Three Fiber | 8.17.x | WebGL canvas |
    55→| drei | 9.114.x | R3F helpers |
    56→| three | 0.170.x | 3D engine |
    57→| BlueprintJS | 6.1.0 | UI components |
    58→| @zazcart/tools | local | State management |
    59→
    60→**Note:** BlueprintJS 6 is the UI component library for this project.
    61→
    62→---
    63→
    64→## MAGNA RULES (Never Violate)
    65→
    66→1. **NO NEXT.JS** - Use Vite + React directly. No SSR needed.
    67→2. **AVOID REACT HOOKS** - Minimize useState/useRef/useEffect. Use @zazcart/tools state-machine.
    68→3. **COMMIT FREQUENTLY** - Small commits > losing work.
    69→4. **60FPS REQUIRED** - Performance is the product differentiator.
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## Project Structure
    74→
    75→```
    76→system/
    77→├── apps/
    78→│   └── canvas/              # Production app (Vite + React 18 + R3F)
    79→│       ├── src/
    80→│       │   ├── main.tsx
    81→│       │   ├── App.tsx
    82→│       │   ├── canvas/      # R3F rendering (InfiniteCanvas, LOD layers)
    83→│       │   ├── components/  # BlueprintJS UI
    84→│       │   ├── data/        # Types, generator
    85→│       │   └── state/       # @zazcart/tools state machine
    86→│       └── package.json
    87→├── docs/
    88→│   └── SPECIFICATION.md     # AUTHORITATIVE specification (2191 lines)
    89→├── progress.txt             # Session history (Harness state)
    90→├── features.json            # Feature status (Harness state)
    91→└── CLAUDE.md                # This file
    92→```
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Specification Reference
    97→
    98→**Full specification:** `docs/SPECIFICATION.md` (2191 lines)
    99→
   100→### Part I: Domain Model
   101→- Railway maintenance (ECM, RAMS, EN 15380)
   102→- Dubai Metro context (128 trains, 2 depots)
   103→- Data model (Train, Task, TaskRender)
   104→- Mathematical formulas (Km Variance, MTBF/MTTR)
   105→
   106→### Part II: Technical Architecture
   107→- State management (@zazcart/tools)
   108→- Canvas (R3F 8.x, OrthographicCamera)
   109→- LOD system (Macro < 0.5, Meso 0.5-2.0, Micro > 2.0)
   110→- Performance (InstancedMesh, RBush, Object Pooling)
   111→
   112→### Part III: User Experience
   113→- Semantic zoom (Google Earth metaphor)
   114→- Visual design (BlueprintJS colors)
   115→- Interactions (pan, zoom, momentum)
   116→- Accessibility (keyboard, ARIA)
   117→
   118→---
   119→
   120→## Key Decisions (from V1.0 Genesis)
   121→
   122→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
   123→|----------|--------|-----------|
   124→| Solver | DEFER to V1.1 | Demo is visualization |
   125→| InstancedMesh | Pre-allocate 100k | Performance |
   126→| RBush | MANDATORY | 100x faster culling |
   127→| Object Pooling | MANDATORY | GC causes frame drops |
   128→| MicroLayer HTML | Limit to 50 | Performance |
   129→
   130→---
   131→
   132→## Performance Targets
   133→
   134→- **60fps** on desktop (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
   135→- **16.67ms** frame budget
   136→- **100k** max instances (pre-allocated)
   137→- **50** max HTML labels (MicroLayer)
   138→- **O(log n)** viewport queries (RBush)
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→## Visual Testing (MCP)
   143→
   144→Use Playwright MCP for visual verification:
   145→
   146→```
   147→1. mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
   148→2. mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
   149→3. mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="test.png"
   150→4. Verify: content visible, no errors, layout correct
   151→5. mcp__playwright__browser_close
   152→```
   153→
   154→**Rule:** Never mark a UI feature complete without visual verification.
   155→
   156→---
   157→
   158→## V1.0 Scope
   159→
   160→### Must Have (Demo Blockers)
   161→- 3-tier Semantic Zoom (Macro/Meso/Micro)
   162→- 60fps Pan/Zoom
   163→- 50 trains x 10 years mock data
   164→- Conflict visualization (red indicators)
   165→- TrainSidebar, Timeline Header
   166→
   167→### Deferred to V1.1
   168→- OR-Tools Solver integration
   169→- Mobile optimization
   170→- Real mileage data
   171→
   172→---
   173→
   174→## Client Context
   175→
   176→**Dubai Metro / Keolis MHI**
   177→- 128 trains (79 Alstom + 49 Kinki Sharyo)
   178→- 2 depots: Al Rashidiya (Red), Jebel Ali (Green)
   179→- 12-year contract
   180→- Maintenance: A-Check (10k km) → B → C → D-Overhaul (800k km)

    88→
    89→# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL
    90→
    91→## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain
    92→
    93→### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)
    94→
    95→The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.
    96→
    97→#### ECM Function Mapping
    98→
    99→| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
   100→|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
   101→| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
   102→| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
   103→| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
   104→| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |
   105→
   106→#### ECM Compliance Requirements
   107→
   108→| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
   109→|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
   110→| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
   111→| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
   112→| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
   113→| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)
   118→
   119→**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety
   120→
   121→EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.
   122→
   123→#### RAMS Metric Definitions
   124→
   125→| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
   126→|--------|------------|------------------|
   127→| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
   128→| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
   129→| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
   130→| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |
   131→
   132→#### RAMS Targets
   133→
   134→| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
   135→|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
   136→| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
   137→| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
   138→| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
   139→| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |
   140→
   141→---
   142→
   143→### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology
   144→
   145→All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.
   146→
   147→#### Mandatory Term Usage
   148→
   149→| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
   150→|---------------|------------|----------------|
   151→| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
   152→| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
   153→| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
   154→| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
   155→| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
   156→| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
   157→| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |
   158→
   159→#### Maintenance Terminology
   160→
   161→| Term | Definition | Context |
   162→|------|------------|---------|
   163→| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
   164→| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
   165→| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
   166→| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
   167→| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |
   168→
   169→---
   170→
   171→## 2. Dubai Metro Context
   172→
   173→### 2.1 Fleet Composition
   174→
   175→| Fleet | Prefix | Count | Line | Depot | Daily Avg km |
   176→|-------|--------|-------|------|-------|--------------|
   177→| Alstom | ALT-001 to ALT-079 | 79 | Red Line | Al Rashidiya | 420-475 km/day |
   178→| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali | 400-454 km/day |
   179→| **Total** | | **128** | | | |
   180→
   181→#### Alstom Fleet Characteristics
   182→
   183→| Property | Value |
   184→|----------|-------|
   185→| Quantity | 79 trains |
   186→| Train Designation | ALT-001 through ALT-079 |
   187→| Cars per Train | 5 |
   188→| Base Mileage Range | 450,000 - 800,000 km |
   189→| Assigned Depot | Al Rashidiya |
   190→
   191→#### Kinki Sharyo Fleet Characteristics
   192→
   193→| Property | Value |
   194→|----------|-------|
   195→| Quantity | 49 trains (newer fleet) |
   196→| Train Designation | KNK-001 through KNK-049 |
   197→| Cars per Train | 5 |
   198→| Base Mileage Range | 320,000 - 600,000 km |
   199→| Assigned Depot | Jebel Ali |
   200→
   201→---
   202→
   203→### 2.2 Depot Configuration
   204→
   205→| Depot ID | Name | Tracks | Weekly Man-Hours | Serves |
   206→|----------|------|--------|------------------|--------|
   207→| depot-red | Al Rashidiya | 5 | 2,800 | Alstom fleet |
   208→| depot-green | Jebel Ali | 4 | 2,400 | Kinki Sharyo fleet |
   209→
   210→#### Combined Resources
   211→
   212→| Resource | Value | Calculation |
   213→|----------|-------|-------------|
   214→| Total Maintenance Tracks | 9 | 5 + 4 |
   215→| Weekly Man-Hours | 5,200 | 2,800 + 2,400 |
   216→| Monthly Man-Hours | 20,800 | 5,200 x 4 weeks |
   217→| Track Capacity (per day) | 2 trains per track | Industry standard |
   218→| Daily Maintenance Capacity | 18 trains | 9 tracks x 2 trains |
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→### 2.3 Contract Scope
   223→
   224→| Parameter | Value |
   225→|-----------|-------|
   226→| Contract Duration | 12 years |
   227→| Operator | Keolis MHI |
   228→| Client | RTA Dubai |
   229→| Total Rolling Stock | 128 trains |
   230→| Planning Horizon | 1-12 years |
   231→| Maintenance Levels | 4 (A/B/C/D checks) |
   232→
   233→---
   234→
   235→## 3. Data Model
   236→
   237→### 3.1 Train Entity
   238→
   239→```typescript
   240→/**
   241→ * Train entity - represents a single rolling stock unit.
   242→ * JSON-serializable for persistence and API transport.
   243→ */
   244→export type AssetType = 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI';
   245→
   246→export interface Train {
   247→  /** Unique identifier: "TRAIN-001" or "ALT-001" */
   248→  id: string;
   249→
   250→  /** Human-readable display name */
   251→  name: string;
   252→
   253→  /**
   254→   * Fleet type for grouping and maintenance profile
   255→   * ALSTOM: Red Line fleet (79 trains)
   256→   * KINKI: Green Line fleet (49 trains)
   257→   */
   258→  assetType: AssetType;
   259→}
   260→
   261→/** Extended Train with optional fields */
   262→export interface TrainExtended extends Train {
   263→  carsCount?: number;
   264→  currentMileageKm?: number;
   265→  avgDailyMileageKm?: number;
   266→  status?: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service';
   267→  depotId?: string;
   268→}
   269→```
   270→
   271→---
   272→
   273→### 3.2 Task Entity
   274→
   275→```typescript
   276→/**
   277→ * Task types for visual categorization.
   278→ * Maps to color: maintenance=blue, overhaul=orange, inspection=gray
   279→ */
   280→export type TaskType = 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
   281→
   282→/**
   283→ * Maintenance level classification (A/B/C/D check system).
   284→ */
   285→export type MaintenanceLevel = 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D';
   286→
   287→/**
   288→ * Task entity - represents a scheduled maintenance activity.
   289→ * Dates stored as ISO 8601 strings, NOT Date objects.
   290→ */
   291→export interface Task {
   292→  /** Unique identifier: "{trainId}-{year}-{index}" */
   293→  id: string;
   294→
   295→  /** Foreign key reference to Train.id */
   296→  trainId: string;
   297→
   298→  /** Task start date in ISO 8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" */
   299→  startDate: string;
   300→
   301→  /** Task end date in ISO 8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" */
   302→  endDate: string;
   303→
   304→  /**
   305→   * Visual category for color coding:
   306→   * - maintenance: Blue (#1d4ed8)
   307→   * - overhaul: Orange (#f59e0b)
   308→   * - inspection: Gray (#6b7280)
   309→   */
   310→  type: TaskType;
   311→
   312→  /**
   313→   * Conflict indicator flag.
   314→   * True if task overlaps another or violates constraints.
   315→   */
   316→  hasConflict: boolean;
   317→
   318→  /** Optional: ID of task that must complete before this one */
   319→  predecessorId?: string;
   320→}
   321→
   322→/** Extended Task with optional fields */
   323→export interface TaskExtended extends Task {
   324→  maintenanceLevel?: MaintenanceLevel;
   325→  manHours?: number;
   326→  estimatedCost?: number;
   327→  dependencies?: string[];
   328→  status?: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled';
   329→  depotId?: string;
   330→  tracksRequired?: number;
   331→}
   332→```
   333→
   334→---
   335→
   336→### 3.3 TaskRender (Computed)
   337→
   338→```typescript
   339→/**
   340→ * Color mapping for task types.
   341→ */
   342→export type TaskColor = 'blue' | 'orange' | 'gray';
   343→
   344→export const TYPE_TO_COLOR: Record<TaskType, TaskColor> = {
   345→  maintenance: 'blue',
   346→  overhaul: 'orange',
   347→  inspection: 'gray',
   348→};
   349→
   350→/**
   351→ * TaskRender - computed values for canvas rendering.
   352→ * NEVER persisted - recomputed on data load.
   353→ */
   354→export interface TaskRender {
   355→  id: string;
   356→  trainIndex: number;     // Y position (0-based index)
   357→  startDay: number;       // X position (days since start)
   358→  duration: number;       // Width in days
   359→  color: TaskColor;       // Derived from type
   360→  hasConflict: boolean;
   361→  type: TaskType;
   362→  predecessorId?: string;
   363→}
   364→
   365→/**
   366→ * Transform persistent Task to render-ready TaskRender.
   367→ */
   368→function transformToRender(
   369→  task: Task,
   370→  trainIndexMap: Map<string, number>,
   371→  datasetStartDate: string
   372→): TaskRender {
   373→  const trainIndex = trainIndexMap.get(task.trainId);
   374→  if (trainIndex === undefined) {
   375→    throw new Error(`Train not found: ${task.trainId}`);
   376→  }
   377→
   378→  const startDay = daysBetween(datasetStartDate, task.startDate);
   379→  const duration = daysBetween(task.startDate, task.endDate);
   380→
   381→  return {
   382→    id: task.id,
   383→    trainIndex,
   384→    startDay,
   385→    duration,
   386→    color: TYPE_TO_COLOR[task.type],
   387→    hasConflict: task.hasConflict,
   388→    type: task.type,
   389→    predecessorId: task.predecessorId,
   390→  };
   391→}
   392→```
   393→
   394→---
   395→
   396→### 3.4 Conflict Representation
   397→
   398→#### Conflict Detection Formula
   399→
   400→```
   401→Overlap Conflict:
   402→  (A.startDay <= B.startDay + B.duration) AND
   403→  (A.startDay + A.duration >= B.startDay) AND
   404→  (A.trainId == B.trainId)
   405→```
   406→
   407→#### Conflict Visualization
   408→
   409→| LOD Level | Conflict Indicator |
   410→|-----------|-------------------|
   411→| MACRO | Red intensity in heatmap cells |
   412→| MESO | Red background on task block |
   413→| MICRO | Red border + warning icon on task card |
   414→
   415→Color specification: Intent.DANGER red (`#cd4246`)
   416→
   417→---
   418→
   419→### 3.5 View Configuration
   420→
   421→```typescript
   422→/**
   423→ * Dataset metadata for world bounds calculation.
   424→ */
   425→export interface DatasetMeta {
   426→  startDate: string;    // ISO 8601: "2024-01-01"
   427→  trainCount: number;   // Number of trains
   428→  years: number;        // Time range in years

   476→---
   477→
   478→## 4. Mathematical Formulas
   479→
   480→### 4.1 Km Variance (Coefficient of Variation)
   481→
   482→Primary metric for fleet mileage balance. Lower variance = better distribution.
   483→
   484→```
   485→Km Variance % = (Standard Deviation / Mean) * 100
   486→
   487→Where:
   488→  mean = sum(all train km) / n
   489→  stddev = sqrt(sum((x_i - mean)^2) / n)
   490→```
   491→
   492→```typescript
   493→function calculateKmVariance(kmValues: number[]): number {
   494→  if (kmValues.length < 2) return 0;
   495→
   496→  const mean = kmValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;
   497→  if (mean === 0) return 0;
   498→
   499→  const squaredDiffs = kmValues.map(km => Math.pow(km - mean, 2));
   500→  const variance = squaredDiffs.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;
   501→  const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);
   502→
   503→  return (stdDev / mean) * 100;
   504→}
   505→```
   506→
   507→| Status | Variance | Indicator |
   508→|--------|----------|-----------|
   509→| Green (Healthy) | <= 10% | Green badge |
   510→| Yellow (Warning) | 10% - 15% | Yellow badge |
   511→| Red (Critical) | > 15% | Red badge |
   512→
   513→---
   514→
   515→### 4.2 Fleet Availability
   516→
   517→```
   518→Fleet Availability % = (Fit Trains / Total Trains) * 100
   519→
   520→Targets:
   521→  Target: >= 97%
   522→  Critical: < 95%
   523→```
   524→
   525→```typescript
   526→function calculateFleetAvailability(fitTrains: number, totalTrains: number): number {
   527→  if (totalTrains === 0) return 0;
   528→  return (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;
   529→}
   530→```
   531→
   532→---
   533→
   534→### 4.3 MTBF / MTTR / MDBF
   535→
   536→```
   537→MTBF = Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures (Hours)
   538→MDBF = Total Operating km / Number of Failures (Kilometers)
   539→MTTR = Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs (Hours)
   540→       Target: <= 24 hours, Critical: > 48 hours
   541→```
   542→
   543→```typescript
   544→interface FailureMetrics {
   545→  mtbf: number;
   546→  mdbf: number;
   547→  mttr: number;
   548→}
   549→
   550→function calculateFailureMetrics(
   551→  operatingHours: number,
   552→  operatingKm: number,
   553→  downtimeHours: number,
   554→  failureCount: number,
   555→  repairCount: number
   556→): FailureMetrics {
   557→  return {
   558→    mtbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingHours / failureCount : Infinity,
   559→    mdbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingKm / failureCount : Infinity,
   560→    mttr: repairCount > 0 ? downtimeHours / repairCount : 0,
   561→  };
   562→}
   563→```
   564→
   565→---
   566→
   567→### 4.4 Coordinate System
   568→
   569→#### Constants
   570→
   571→| Constant | Value | Description |
   572→|----------|-------|-------------|
   573→| `PX_PER_DAY` | 10 pixels | 1 day = 10 pixels on X axis |
   574→| `PX_PER_TRAIN` | 24 pixels | 1 train row = 24 pixels on Y axis |
   575→| `minZoom` | 0.1 | Minimum zoom (zoomed out) |
   576→| `maxZoom` | 5.0 | Maximum zoom (zoomed in) |
   577→
   578→#### Coordinate Conversions
   579→
   580→```typescript
   581→const PX_PER_DAY = 10;
   582→const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24;
   583→
   584→function dayToX(day: number): number {
   585→  return day * PX_PER_DAY;
   586→}
   587→
   588→function trainIndexToY(index: number): number {
   589→  return -index * PX_PER_TRAIN;
   590→}
   591→
   592→function screenToWorld(
   593→  screenX: number, screenY: number,
   594→  cameraX: number, cameraY: number,
   595→  zoom: number,
   596→  viewportWidth: number, viewportHeight: number
   597→): { worldX: number; worldY: number } {
   598→  return {
   599→    worldX: cameraX + (screenX - viewportWidth / 2) / zoom,
   600→    worldY: cameraY + (screenY - viewportHeight / 2) / zoom,
   601→  };
   602→}
   603→```
   604→
   605→#### LOD Thresholds
   606→
   607→| LOD Level | Zoom Range | Time Scale | Rendering |
   608→|-----------|------------|------------|-----------|
   609→| MACRO | < 0.5 | 10-20 years | Heatmap (aggregated) |
   610→| MESO | 0.5 - 2.0 | 2-5 years | Task blocks (instanced) |
   611→| MICRO | >= 2.0 | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards (HTML) |
   612→
   613→#### Blend Zone
   614→
   615→```typescript
   616→const BLEND_ZONE_SIZE = 0.15;
   617→
   618→function calculateOpacity(
   619→  zoom: number,
   620→  threshold: number,
   621→  direction: 'in' | 'out'
   622→): number {
   623→  const t = (zoom - threshold) / BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;
   624→  const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, t));
   625→  return direction === 'in' ? clamped : 1 - clamped;
   626→}
   627→```
   628→
   629→---
   630→
   631→### 4.5 Km Penalty Calculation (Solver)
   632→
   633→Used by allocation solver to prioritize under-utilized trains.
   634→
   635→```
   636→deviation_pct = |current_km - target_km| / max(target_km, 1) * 100
   637→
   638→If current_km < target_km:
   639→  penalty = -deviation_pct * 10  (BONUS)
   640→If current_km >= target_km:
   641→  penalty = +deviation_pct * 10  (PENALTY)
   642→```
   643→
   644→```typescript
   645→function calculateKmPenalty(
   646→  currentKm: number,
   647→  targetKm: number,
   648→  fleetAvgKm: number
   649→): number {
   650→  const effectiveTarget = targetKm || fleetAvgKm;
   651→  if (effectiveTarget === 0) return 0;
   652→
   653→  const deviationPct = Math.abs(currentKm - effectiveTarget) / effectiveTarget * 100;
   654→
   655→  if (currentKm < effectiveTarget) {
   656→    return -Math.round(deviationPct * 10);
   657→  } else {
   658→    return Math.round(deviationPct * 10);
   659→  }
   660→}
   661→```
   662→
   663→---
   664→
   665→### 4.6 Haversine Distance (Dead Running)
   666→
   667→Great circle distance between two geographic points.
   668→
   669→```
   670→a = sin^2(delta_lat/2) + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin^2(delta_lon/2)
   671→c = 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1-a))
   672→distance = R * c
   673→
   674→Where: R = 6371 km (Earth's radius)
   675→```
   676→
   677→```typescript
   678→function haversineDistance(
   679→  lat1: number, lon1: number,
   680→  lat2: number, lon2: number
   681→): number {
   682→  const R = 6371;
   683→  const toRad = (deg: number) => deg * Math.PI / 180;
   684→
   685→  const lat1Rad = toRad(lat1);
   686→  const lat2Rad = toRad(lat2);
   687→  const deltaLat = toRad(lat2 - lat1);
   688→  const deltaLon = toRad(lon2 - lon1);
   689→
   690→  const a = Math.sin(deltaLat / 2) ** 2 +
   691→            Math.cos(lat1Rad) * Math.cos(lat2Rad) *
   692→            Math.sin(deltaLon / 2) ** 2;
   693→
   694→  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a));
   695→
   696→  return R * c;
   697→}
   698→```
   699→

   702→## 5. Maintenance Level Mapping
   703→
   704→### Authoritative Mapping Table
   705→
   706→| Code | Railway Term | Display | Interval (km) | Tolerance | Duration | Man-Hours | Tracks | Cost |
   707→|------|--------------|---------|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------|--------|------|
   708→| A | PM1 | A-Check | 10,000 | +/- 2,000 | 4h | 8 | 1 | $2,500 |
   709→| B | PM2 | B-Check | 50,000 | +/- 5,000 | 16h | 32 | 1 | $8,500 |
   710→| C | PM3 | C-Check | 200,000 | +/- 20,000 | 72h | 160 | 1 | $45,000 |
   711→| D | HM | D-Overhaul | 800,000 | +/- 50,000 | 480h | 1,200 | 2 | $180,000 |
   712→
   713→#### Terminology Key
   714→
   715→| Term | Meaning |
   716→|------|---------|
   717→| **PM1** | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (Light) |
   718→| **PM2** | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (Intermediate) |
   719→| **PM3** | Preventive Maintenance Level 3 (Scheduled) |
   720→| **HM** | Heavy Maintenance / Major Overhaul |
   721→| **OVH** | Full Overhaul / Refurbishment (resets all counters) |
   722→
   723→### Maintenance Cascade
   724→
   725→```
   726→Fleet Type (e.g., Alstom Red Line)
   727→ |
   728→ +-- Maintenance Profile
   729→     |
   730→     +-- PM1 / A-Check (every 10,000 km)
   731→     |   Duration: 4 hours
   732→     |
   733→     +-- PM2 / B-Check (every 50,000 km)
   734→     |   Duration: 16 hours, INCLUDES PM1 tasks
   735→     |
   736→     +-- PM3 / C-Check (every 200,000 km)
   737→     |   Duration: 72 hours, INCLUDES PM1 + PM2 tasks
   738→     |
   739→     +-- HM / D-Overhaul (every 800,000 km)
   740→         Duration: 480 hours, RESETS counters
   741→```
   742→
   743→### Maintenance Triggers
   744→
   745→| Trigger Type | Description | Best For |
   746→|--------------|-------------|----------|
   747→| Time-based | Calendar intervals | Simple assets, regulatory |
   748→| Mileage-based | Odometer intervals (primary) | Wear-dependent components |
   749→| Condition-based | Sensor thresholds | Critical components |
   750→| Hybrid | Combined triggers | Most applications |
   751→

   754→## 6. Domain Rules
   755→
   756→### 6.1 Hard Constraints (Never Violate)
   757→
   758→| ID | Rule | Rationale |
   759→|----|------|-----------|
   760→| HC-1 | Unfit trains NEVER assigned to service | Safety-critical |
   761→| HC-2 | Maintenance windows are immutable | Planned downtime |
   762→| HC-3 | Aptitude requirements are binary | Route compatibility |
   763→| HC-4 | Depot capacity is physical | Track slots |
   764→| HC-5 | Service cannot be cancelled for balancing | Operations > Optimization |
   765→
   766→### 6.2 Soft Constraints (Optimize Within)
   767→
   768→| ID | Rule | Weight |
   769→|----|------|--------|
   770→| SC-1 | Km balancing priority | 1000 (HIGH) |
   771→| SC-2 | Dead running minimization | 100 (MEDIUM) |
   772→| SC-3 | Maintenance depot proximity | 10 (LOW) |
   773→
   774→### 6.3 Tolerance Windows
   775→
   776→| Level | Tolerance | Overrun Warning | Critical |
   777→|-------|-----------|-----------------|----------|
   778→| PM1 / A-Check | +/- 2,000 km | 95% | 105% |
   779→| PM2 / B-Check | +/- 5,000 km | 95% | 105% |
   780→| PM3 / C-Check | +/- 20,000 km | 95% | 105% |
   781→| HM / D-Overhaul | +/- 50,000 km | 95% | 105% |

   842→### Time Zone Concept (Frozen Periods)
   843→
   844→| Horizon | Zone | Constraint | Allowed Changes |
   845→|---------|------|------------|-----------------|
   846→| T > 12 weeks | **Liquid** | Soft | Structure, Volume, Frequency |
   847→| T-12 to T-7 | **Slushy** | Medium | Timing, Resource Allocation |
   848→| T-7 to T-48h | **Frozen** | Hard | Emergency Only |
   849→| T < 48h | **Execution** | Fixed | Break-fix only |

   937→
   938→## 10. State Management
   939→
   940→### 10.1 Library Overview
   941→
   942→**Package:** `@zazcart/tools` (located at `packages/toolkit`)
   943→
   944→The library provides a slice-based state machine pattern with:
   945→- Immer-powered immutable updates
   946→- React 18 `useSyncExternalStore` integration
   947→- Zero-allocation option for 60fps updates
   948→
   949→**Note:** `@zazcart/tools` and `@belt/tools` are the SAME library.
   950→
   951→### 10.2 createMachine Pattern
   952→
   953→```typescript
   954→// File: apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts
   955→import { createMachine } from '@zazcart/tools';
   956→import { viewportSlice } from './viewport';
   957→import { interactionSlice } from './interaction';
   958→
   959→export const canvasMachine = createMachine({
   960→  id: 'canvas',
   961→  slices: {
   962→    viewport: viewportSlice,
   963→    interaction: interactionSlice,
   964→  },
   965→  context: {
   966→    userID: null,
   967→  },
   968→  // CRITICAL: Disable metadata tracking for 60fps
   969→  metadata: false,
   970→});
   971→
   972→export const useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;
   973→export const useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;
   974→export const canvasActions = canvasMachine.actions;
   975→
   976→export const canvasStore = {
   977→  getState: canvasMachine.getState,
   978→  dispatch: canvasMachine.dispatch,
   979→  subscribe: canvasMachine.subscribe,
   980→};
   981→```
   982→
   983→### 10.3 Slice Definitions
   984→
   985→#### Viewport Slice
   986→
   987→```typescript
   988→// File: apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts
   989→import { createSyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';
   990→
   991→export interface CameraPosition {
   992→  x: number;
   993→  y: number;
   994→  zoom: number;
   995→}
   996→
   997→export interface ViewportSliceState {
   998→  camera: CameraPosition;
   999→  canvasSize: { width: number; height: number };
  1000→  isInitialized: boolean;
  1001→  momentum: { velocityX: number; velocityY: number; isActive: boolean };
  1002→  animation: {
  1003→    isAnimating: boolean;
  1004→    startTime: number;
  1005→    duration: number;
  1006→    startX: number; startY: number; startZoom: number;
  1007→    targetX: number; targetY: number; targetZoom: number;
  1008→  };
  1009→  isPanning: boolean;
  1010→  panStart: CameraPosition;
  1011→  cameraPosAtPanStart: CameraPosition;
  1012→}
  1013→
  1014→export const viewportSlice = createSyncSlice({
  1015→  id: 'viewport',
  1016→  initial: {
  1017→    camera: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },
  1018→    canvasSize: { width: 0, height: 0 },
  1019→    isInitialized: false,
  1020→    momentum: { velocityX: 0, velocityY: 0, isActive: false },
  1021→    animation: {
  1022→      isAnimating: false, startTime: 0, duration: 250,
  1023→      startX: 0, startY: 0, startZoom: 1,
  1024→      targetX: 0, targetY: 0, targetZoom: 1,
  1025→    },
  1026→    isPanning: false,
  1027→    panStart: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },
  1028→    cameraPosAtPanStart: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },
  1029→  },
  1030→  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  1031→  reducers: {
  1032→    setCamera(state, payload: CameraPosition) { state.camera = payload; },
  1033→    setZoom(state, payload: number) { state.camera.zoom = payload; },
  1034→    setCanvasSize(state, payload: { width: number; height: number }) {
  1035→      state.canvasSize = payload;
  1036→      state.isInitialized = true;
  1037→    },
  1038→    startPan(state, payload: { x: number; y: number }) {
  1039→      state.isPanning = true;
  1040→      state.panStart = { x: payload.x, y: payload.y, zoom: state.camera.zoom };
  1041→      state.cameraPosAtPanStart = { ...state.camera };
  1042→    },
  1043→    endPan(state) { state.isPanning = false; },
  1044→    startMomentum(state, payload: { velocityX: number; velocityY: number }) {
  1045→      state.momentum = { ...payload, isActive: true };
  1046→    },
  1047→    stopMomentum(state) {
  1048→      state.momentum = { velocityX: 0, velocityY: 0, isActive: false };
  1049→    },
  1050→    reset() { return viewportSlice.initial; },
  1051→  },
  1052→});
  1053→```
  1054→
  1055→#### Interaction Slice

  1092→### 10.4 Usage Patterns
  1093→
  1094→**Pattern 1: React Component with Selector**
  1095→
  1096→```typescript
  1097→import { useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore } from '../state';
  1098→
  1099→function ZoomControls() {
  1100→  const zoom = useCanvasSelector(s => s.viewport.camera.zoom);
  1101→
  1102→  const handleZoomIn = () => {
  1103→    canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setZoom(zoom * 1.2));
  1104→  };

  1107→    <button onClick={handleZoomIn}>+</button>
  1108→  );
  1109→}

  1111→
  1112→**Pattern 2: Animation Loop (NO React Hooks)**
  1113→
  1114→```typescript
  1115→import { useFrame } from '@react-three/fiber';
  1116→import { canvasStore, canvasActions } from '../state';
  1117→
  1118→function CameraAnimator() {
  1119→  useFrame(() => {
  1120→    // CORRECT: Get fresh state every frame
  1121→    const state = canvasStore.getState();
  1122→    const { animation, camera } = state.viewport;
  1123→
  1124→    if (!animation.isAnimating) return;
  1125→
  1126→    const elapsed = Date.now() - animation.startTime;
  1127→    const progress = Math.min(elapsed / animation.duration, 1);
  1128→
  1129→    // Update camera...
  1130→  });
  1131→
  1132→  return null;
  1133→}
  1134→```
  1135→
  1136→**Pattern 3: Event Handler**
  1137→
  1138→```typescript
  1139→const handleWheel = useCallback((event: WheelEvent) => {
  1140→  event.preventDefault();
  1141→  const state = canvasStore.getState();
  1142→  const { camera } = state.viewport;
  1143→
  1144→  const zoomDelta = -event.deltaY * ZOOM_SENSITIVITY;
  1145→  const newZoom = clamp(camera.zoom * (1 + zoomDelta), MIN_ZOOM, MAX_ZOOM);
  1146→
  1147→  canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setCamera({
  1148→    x: camera.x, y: camera.y, zoom: newZoom,
  1149→  }));
  1150→}, []);
  1151→```

  1155→## 11. Canvas Architecture
  1156→
  1157→### 11.1 React Three Fiber Integration
  1158→
  1159→**Version Pairing (Critical):**
  1160→
  1161→| React Version | R3F Version | drei Version |
  1162→|---------------|-------------|--------------|
  1163→| **React 18** | **R3F 8.x** | **drei 9.x** |
  1164→| React 19 | R3F 9.x | drei 10.x |
  1165→
  1166→```typescript
  1167→// Camera configuration
  1168→const CAMERA_NEAR = -1000;
  1169→const CAMERA_FAR = 1000;
  1170→const INITIAL_ZOOM = 1;
  1171→
  1172→<Canvas
  1173→  orthographic
  1174→  camera={{
  1175→    position: [0, 0, 100],
  1176→    zoom: INITIAL_ZOOM,
  1177→    near: CAMERA_NEAR,
  1178→    far: CAMERA_FAR,
  1179→  }}
  1180→  gl={{
  1181→    antialias: true,
  1182→    alpha: false,
  1183→    powerPreference: 'high-performance',
  1184→  }}
  1185→>
  1186→  <CameraController />
  1187→  <LODLayers />
  1188→</Canvas>
  1189→```
  1190→
  1191→### 11.2 Coordinate System
  1192→
  1193→```
  1194→                                    +X (Time - Days)
  1195→                                    |
  1196→        (-) Past                    |                    (+) Future
  1197→   <--------------------------------+--------------------------------->
  1198→                                    |
  1199→                   Day 0 = meta.startDate (2024-01-01)
  1200→
  1201→+Y (Trains - negative because canvas Y grows down)
  1202→|
  1203→|  Train 0:  y = 0
  1204→|  Train 1:  y = -24 (PX_PER_TRAIN)
  1205→|  Train 2:  y = -48
  1206→|  ...
  1207→|  Train 49: y = -1176
  1208→v
  1209→
  1210→Constants:
  1211→  PX_PER_DAY = 10
  1212→  PX_PER_TRAIN = 24
  1213→
  1214→Viewport:
  1215→  Camera position (x, y) is CENTER of visible area
  1216→  Visible width = canvasWidth / zoom
  1217→  Visible height = canvasHeight / zoom
  1218→```
  1219→
  1220→---
  1221→
  1222→## 12. LOD System
  1223→
  1224→### 12.1 Three-Layer Architecture
  1225→
  1226→| Layer | Zoom Range | Visualization | Max Instances |
  1227→|-------|------------|---------------|---------------|
  1228→| **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap cells | 600 |
  1229→| **MESO** | 0.5x - 2.0x | Task blocks | 100,000 |
  1230→| **MICRO** | > 2.0x | HTML labels + curves | 50 labels |
  1231→
  1232→### 12.2 Crossfade Transitions
  1233→
  1234→```typescript
  1235→const LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
  1236→  macroToMeso: 0.5,
  1237→  mesoToMicro: 2.0,
  1238→};
  1239→const BLEND_ZONE_SIZE = 0.15;
  1240→
  1241→function calculateLayerOpacities(zoom: number): {
  1242→  macro: number; meso: number; micro: number;
  1243→} {
  1244→  const { macroToMeso, mesoToMicro } = LOD_THRESHOLDS;
  1245→
  1246→  const macroEnd = macroToMeso - BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;
  1247→  const mesoStart = macroToMeso + BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;
  1248→  const mesoEnd = mesoToMicro - BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;
  1249→  const microStart = mesoToMicro + BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;
  1250→
  1251→  let macro = 0, meso = 0, micro = 0;
  1252→
  1253→  if (zoom <= macroEnd) macro = 1;
  1254→  else if (zoom < mesoStart) {
  1255→    const t = (zoom - macroEnd) / (mesoStart - macroEnd);
  1256→    macro = 1 - t;
  1257→    meso = t;
  1258→  } else if (zoom <= mesoEnd) {
  1259→    meso = 1;
  1260→  } else if (zoom < microStart) {
  1261→    const t = (zoom - mesoEnd) / (microStart - mesoEnd);
  1262→    meso = 1 - t;
  1263→    micro = t;
  1264→  } else {
  1265→    micro = 1;
  1266→  }
  1267→
  1268→  return { macro, meso, micro };
  1269→}
  1270→```
  1271→
  1272→**Material Configuration:**
  1273→
  1274→```typescript
  1275→<meshBasicMaterial
  1276→  toneMapped={false}
  1277→  transparent={opacity < 1}
  1278→  opacity={opacity}
  1279→  depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5}  // Prevent z-fighting
  1280→/>
  1281→```
  1282→
  1283→### 12.3 Layer Implementations
  1284→
  1285→#### MacroLayer
  1286→
  1287→```typescript
  1288→// InstancedMesh with heatmap coloring
  1289→const CELL_WIDTH = 30 * 10;  // ~30 days * pxPerDay
  1290→const CELL_HEIGHT = 24;
  1291→
  1292→export function MacroLayer({ tasks, trainCount, opacity = 1 }) {
  1293→  const meshRef = useRef<InstancedMesh>(null);
  1294→  const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);
  1295→  const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);
  1296→
  1297→  const totalMonths = 10 * 12;
  1298→  const totalCells = trainCount * totalMonths;
  1299→
  1300→  const densityGrid = useMemo(
  1301→    () => calculateDensityGrid(tasks, trainCount, totalMonths),
  1302→    [tasks, trainCount, totalMonths]
  1303→  );
  1304→
  1305→  useEffect(() => {
  1306→    if (!meshRef.current) return;
  1307→    // Update instance matrices and colors based on density...
  1308→  }, [densityGrid]);
  1309→
  1310→  return (
  1311→    <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[undefined, undefined, totalCells]}>
  1312→      <planeGeometry args={[1, 1]} />
  1313→      <meshBasicMaterial toneMapped={false} transparent={opacity < 1}
  1314→        opacity={opacity} depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5} vertexColors />
  1315→    </instancedMesh>
  1316→  );
  1317→}
  1318→```
  1319→
  1320→#### MesoLayer
  1321→
  1322→```typescript
  1323→const MAX_INSTANCES = 100000;
  1324→const TASK_HEIGHT = 18;
  1325→
  1326→const COLORS = {
  1327→  blue: '#3b82f6',
  1328→  orange: '#f59e0b',
  1329→  gray: '#9ca3af',
  1330→};
  1331→const CONFLICT_COLOR = '#ef4444';
  1332→
  1333→export function MesoLayer({ tasks, opacity = 1 }) {
  1334→  const meshRef = useRef<InstancedMesh>(null);
  1335→  const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);
  1336→  const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);
  1337→
  1338→  useEffect(() => {
  1339→    if (!meshRef.current) return;
  1340→    meshRef.current.count = tasks.length;
  1341→
  1342→    tasks.forEach((task, i) => {
  1343→      const width = task.duration * PX_PER_DAY;
  1344→      const x = task.startDay * PX_PER_DAY + width / 2;
  1345→      const y = -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN - PX_PER_TRAIN / 2;
  1346→
  1347→      tempObject.position.set(x, y, 0);
  1348→      tempObject.scale.set(width, TASK_HEIGHT, 1);
  1349→      tempObject.updateMatrix();
  1350→      meshRef.current.setMatrixAt(i, tempObject.matrix);
  1351→
  1352→      const color = task.hasConflict ? CONFLICT_COLOR : COLORS[task.color];
  1353→      tempColor.set(color);
  1354→      meshRef.current.setColorAt(i, tempColor);
  1355→    });
  1356→
  1357→    meshRef.current.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true;
  1358→    if (meshRef.current.instanceColor) {
  1359→      meshRef.current.instanceColor.needsUpdate = true;
  1360→    }
  1361→  }, [tasks]);
  1362→
  1363→  return (
  1364→    <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[undefined, undefined, MAX_INSTANCES]}>
  1365→      <planeGeometry args={[1, 1]} />
  1366→      <meshBasicMaterial toneMapped={false} transparent={opacity < 1}
  1367→        opacity={opacity} depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5} vertexColors />
  1368→    </instancedMesh>
  1369→  );
  1370→}
  1371→```
  1372→
  1373→#### MicroLayer
  1374→
  1375→```typescript
  1376→const MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS = 50;
  1377→
  1378→export function MicroLayer({ tasks, viewportBounds, opacity = 1 }) {
  1379→  const visibleTasks = useMemo(() => {
  1380→    if (!viewportBounds) return tasks.slice(0, MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS);
  1381→    const visible = spatialIndex.queryViewport(viewportBounds);
  1382→    return visible.slice(0, MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS);
  1383→  }, [tasks, viewportBounds]);
  1384→
  1385→  return (
  1386→    <group>
  1387→      <TaskBlocks tasks={tasks} opacity={opacity} />
  1388→      {visibleTasks.map(task => (
  1389→        <TaskLabel key={task.id} task={task} opacity={opacity} />
  1390→      ))}
  1391→      <DependencyLines tasks={visibleTasks} opacity={opacity} />
  1392→    </group>
  1393→  );
  1394→}
  1395→```

  1398→
  1399→## 13. Performance Architecture
  1400→
  1401→### 13.1 Performance Requirements
  1402→
  1403→| Metric | Target | Confidence |
  1404→|--------|--------|------------|
  1405→| **Frame Rate** | 60fps constant | 70% (desktop) |
  1406→| **Frame Budget** | < 16.67ms | 70% (desktop) |
  1407→| **Viewport Query** | < 1ms | 95% (verified) |
  1408→| **LOD Transition** | < 100ms | 90% |
  1409→| **Memory** | < 100MB stable | 80% |
  1410→
  1411→### 13.2 InstancedMesh Pre-allocation
  1412→
  1413→**Problem:** Dynamic count causes GPU buffer reallocation.
  1414→
  1415→```typescript
  1416→// WRONG: Recreates buffer when length changes
  1417→<instancedMesh args={[geometry, material, tasks.length]}>
  1418→
  1419→// CORRECT: Pre-allocate, control with mesh.count
  1420→const MAX_INSTANCES = 100000;
  1421→<instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[geometry, material, MAX_INSTANCES]}>
  1422→
  1423→// In useEffect:
  1424→mesh.count = actualTaskCount;
  1425→```
  1426→
  1427→**Memory Cost:** 100k instances x 64 bytes = ~6.4MB GPU memory
  1428→
  1429→### 13.3 RBush Spatial Indexing
  1430→
  1431→**MANDATORY: All viewport queries must use RBush. Array.filter() is FORBIDDEN.**
  1432→
  1433→```typescript
  1434→import RBush from 'rbush';
  1435→
  1436→interface RBushItem {
  1437→  minX: number; minY: number;
  1438→  maxX: number; maxY: number;
  1439→  id: string;
  1440→}
  1441→
  1442→export class RBushSpatialIndex {
  1443→  private tree: RBush<RBushItem>;
  1444→  private itemsById: Map<string, RBushItem> = new Map();
  1445→
  1446→  constructor() { this.tree = new RBush(); }
  1447→
  1448→  load(tasks: TaskRender[]): void {
  1449→    this.tree.clear();
  1450→    this.itemsById.clear();
  1451→
  1452→    const items: RBushItem[] = tasks.map(task => ({
  1453→      minX: task.startDay * PX_PER_DAY,
  1454→      maxX: (task.startDay + task.duration) * PX_PER_DAY,
  1455→      minY: -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN - PX_PER_TRAIN,
  1456→      maxY: -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN,
  1457→      id: task.id,
  1458→    }));
  1459→
  1460→    for (const item of items) {
  1461→      this.itemsById.set(item.id, item);
  1462→    }
  1463→
  1464→    this.tree.load(items);  // Bulk load O(n log n)
  1465→  }
  1466→
  1467→  queryViewport(bounds: ViewportBounds): string[] {
  1468→    const OVERSCAN = 100;
  1469→    const results = this.tree.search({
  1470→      minX: bounds.minX - OVERSCAN,
  1471→      maxX: bounds.maxX + OVERSCAN,
  1472→      minY: bounds.minY - OVERSCAN,
  1473→      maxY: bounds.maxY + OVERSCAN,
  1474→    });
  1475→    return results.map(item => item.id);
  1476→  }
  1477→
  1478→  queryNearest(x: number, y: number, maxDistance = 50): { id: string; distance: number } | null {
  1479→    let radius = 20;
  1480→    let nearest: { id: string; distance: number } | null = null;
  1481→
  1482→    while (radius <= maxDistance) {
  1483→      const candidates = this.tree.search({
  1484→        minX: x - radius, maxX: x + radius,
  1485→        minY: y - radius, maxY: y + radius,
  1486→      });
  1487→
  1488→      for (const item of candidates) {
  1489→        const centerX = (item.minX + item.maxX) / 2;
  1490→        const centerY = (item.minY + item.maxY) / 2;
  1491→        const dist = Math.sqrt((x - centerX) ** 2 + (y - centerY) ** 2);
  1492→
  1493→        if (dist <= maxDistance && (!nearest || dist < nearest.distance)) {
  1494→          nearest = { id: item.id, distance: dist };
  1495→        }
  1496→      }
  1497→
  1498→      if (nearest) return nearest;
  1499→      radius *= 2;
  1500→    }
  1501→
  1502→    return nearest;
  1503→  }
  1504→}
  1505→```
  1506→
  1507→### 13.4 Object Pooling
  1508→
  1509→**MANDATORY: GC is the primary bottleneck in WebGL applications.**
  1510→
  1511→```typescript
  1512→// In component scope (created once, reused every frame)
  1513→const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);
  1514→const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);
  1515→const tempVector = useMemo(() => new Vector3(), []);
  1516→
  1517→// In animation loop
  1518→useFrame(() => {
  1519→  // CORRECT: Reuse pooled objects
  1520→  tempVector.set(x, y, z);
  1521→  ref.current.position.copy(tempVector);
  1522→
  1523→  // WRONG: Creates new object every frame
  1524→  // ref.current.position.copy(new Vector3(x, y, z));
  1525→});
  1526→```
  1527→
  1528→### 13.5 GC Avoidance
  1529→
  1530→| Strategy | Implementation | Impact |
  1531→|----------|----------------|--------|
  1532→| Object Pooling | useMemo for temp objects | High |
  1533→| metadata: false | State machine config | Medium |
  1534→| TypedArrays | EntityStore SoA pattern | High |
  1535→| Pre-allocation | InstancedMesh MAX_INSTANCES | High |
  1536→| Bulk operations | RBush.load() | Medium |
  1537→
  1538→**Frame Budget: 16.67ms**
  1539→
  1540→| Operation | Budget | Priority |
  1541→|-----------|--------|----------|
  1542→| GPU Render | ~8ms | Critical |
  1543→| JavaScript | ~5ms | Critical |
  1544→| Layout/Paint | ~2ms | Low |
  1545→| GC | **0ms target** | **Eliminate** |
  1546→

  1549→## 14. Data Flow
  1550→
  1551→### Data Generation Pipeline
  1552→
  1553→```
  1554→+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
  1555→|                      DATA GENERATION PIPELINE                      |
  1556→+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
  1557→
  1558→+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+
  1559→|   Generator      | --> |   State Store    | --> |   Render Layer   |
  1560→|   (mock-data.ts) |     |   (canvasStore)  |     |   (LOD Layers)   |
  1561→+--------+---------+     +--------+---------+     +--------+---------+
  1562→         |                        |                        |
  1563→         v                        v                        v
  1564→+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+
  1565→| Persistent Types |     | Runtime State    |     | GPU Buffers      |
  1566→| - Train          |     | - ViewportState  |     | - InstanceMatrix |
  1567→| - Task           |     | - Interaction    |     | - InstanceColor  |
  1568→| - DatasetMeta    |     | - EntityStore    |     | - HTML overlays  |
  1569→+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+
  1570→```
  1571→
  1572→### Viewport Culling Flow
  1573→
  1574→```
  1575→1. User pans/zooms canvas
  1576→        |
  1577→        v
  1578→2. Camera position updates in state
  1579→        |
  1580→        v
  1581→3. Calculate viewport bounds in world coordinates
  1582→        |
  1583→        v
  1584→4. Query RBush spatial index (O(log n + k))
  1585→        |
  1586→        v
  1587→5. Map IDs to TaskRender objects
  1588→        |
  1589→        v
  1590→6. Update InstancedMesh:
  1591→   mesh.count = visibleTasks.length
  1592→   for each task: setMatrixAt, setColorAt
  1593→   mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true
  1594→```

  1598→# PART III: USER EXPERIENCE
  1599→
  1600→## 15. Semantic Zoom Concept
  1601→
  1602→### Core Metaphor
  1603→
  1604→**"Google Earth for Time"** - The visualization transforms based on zoom level.
  1605→
  1606→```
  1607→   ZOOM OUT (Macro)              ZOOM LEVEL               ZOOM IN (Micro)
  1608→        <---------------------------------------------------------------->
  1609→
  1610→   +-------------+          +-------------+          +-------------+
  1611→   | ░░▓▓░░░░░░ |          | ▓▓▓ ░░░ ▓▓ |          | +--------+ |
  1612→   | ░░░▓▓▓░░░░ |          | ░░ ▓▓▓ ░░░ |          | | Task A | |
  1613→   | ░▓▓▓░░░░░░ |          | ▓▓ ░░░ ▓▓▓ |          | | 8h     | |
  1614→   +-------------+          +-------------+          +-------------+
  1615→
  1616→   12-YEAR HEATMAP          2-YEAR DNA STRIP        4-WEEK GANTT
  1617→   (Strategic)               (Tactical)              (Operational)
  1618→```
  1619→
  1620→| Zoom Level | Data Representation | User Question |
  1621→|------------|---------------------|---------------|

  1737→## 18. Interaction Patterns
  1738→
  1739→### 18.1 Pan Navigation
  1740→
  1741→```
  1742→User drags from A to B
  1743→
  1744→        A ----------------> B
  1745→
  1746→Canvas moves in OPPOSITE direction
  1747→(content appears to move with drag)
  1748→```
  1749→
  1750→**Constraints:**
  1751→- Unconstrained within data bounds
  1752→- Soft-stop at edges with 10% overscan
  1753→- Snap-back animation if dragged beyond
  1754→
  1755→### 18.2 Zoom Interaction
  1756→
  1757→```
  1758→Scroll UP (away from user)    ->    Zoom IN (more detail)
  1759→Scroll DOWN (toward user)     ->    Zoom OUT (less detail)
  1760→
  1761→Zoom anchored at cursor position
  1762→```
  1763→
  1764→**Zoom Range:**
  1765→
  1766→| Constant | Value | Purpose |
  1767→|----------|-------|---------|
  1768→| MIN_ZOOM | 0.1 | 20 years visible |
  1769→| MAX_ZOOM | 5.0 | 1 week visible |
  1770→| DEFAULT_ZOOM | 1.0 | 5 years visible |
  1771→
  1772→### 18.3 Gesture Mapping
  1773→
  1774→| Gesture | Device | Action |
  1775→|---------|--------|--------|
  1776→| Left-drag | Mouse | Pan canvas |
  1777→| Scroll wheel | Mouse | Zoom at cursor |
  1778→| Two-finger scroll | Trackpad | Pan canvas |
  1779→| Pinch | Trackpad/Touch | Zoom at center |
  1780→| Ctrl + Scroll | All | Horizontal pan |
  1781→| Shift + Scroll | All | Vertical pan |
  1782→| Double-click | All | Zoom in 2x |
  1783→
  1784→### 18.4 Momentum Physics
  1785→
  1786→```typescript
  1787→const FRICTION = 0.93;
  1788→const MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5;
  1789→
  1790→function startMomentum(initialVelocity: { x: number; y: number }) {
  1791→  let velocity = { ...initialVelocity };
  1792→
  1793→  function animate() {
  1794→    velocity.x *= FRICTION;
  1795→    velocity.y *= FRICTION;
  1796→
  1797→    pan(velocity.x, velocity.y);
  1798→
  1799→    const magnitude = Math.sqrt(velocity.x ** 2 + velocity.y ** 2);
  1800→    if (magnitude > MIN_VELOCITY) {
  1801→      requestAnimationFrame(animate);
  1802→    }
  1803→  }
  1804→
  1805→  requestAnimationFrame(animate);
  1806→}
  1807→```

  2098→
  2099→## Appendix A: Decision Log
  2100→
  2101→| ID | Area | Decision | Rationale |
  2102→|----|------|----------|-----------|
  2103→| DECISION-001 | Solver | Defer to V1.1 | Demo is visualization, solver adds backend complexity |
  2104→| DECISION-002 | UI Framework | Keep BlueprintJS 6 | Working, cosmetic change not worth risk |
  2105→| DECISION-003 | Multi-Depot | Include in data model | Client has 2 depots, visual only |
  2106→| DECISION-004 | Km Variance | Use 10%/15% thresholds | Conservative values from domain docs |
  2107→| DECISION-005 | Audit Trail | Defer to V1.1 | Requires backend, not demo differentiator |
  2108→| DECISION-006 | Cascade Display | Data only, no visual | Complex UI, data model sufficient |
  2109→| DECISION-007 | InstancedMesh | Pre-allocate 100k | Avoid GPU buffer reallocation |
  2110→| DECISION-008 | Viewport Query | RBush MANDATORY | 100x faster than Array.filter |
  2111→| DECISION-009 | GC Prevention | Object pooling | GC is primary bottleneck |
  2112→| DECISION-010 | MicroLayer | Max 50 labels | HTML is expensive |
  2113→| DECISION-011 | LOD Transition | depthWrite: false | Prevent z-fighting |
  2114→| DECISION-012 | Timeline | 3 weeks to demo | Balance performance and features |
